reasoning: I will provide a concise, neutral summary of the article, focusing on the key facts, timeline, and statements from the professionals involved, preserving the inquest context.
output: Iwan Wyn Lewis, a 36-year-old from Penygroes, Gwynedd, had been sectioned at Ysbyty Gwynedd after allegedly assaulting his mother. In April 2016 an officer visited him on the Cynan ward about his bail arrangements; a doctor said the visit could have added to his distress. His body was found in the Menai Strait on 2 May. He had been receiving treatment at the hospital’s Hergest unit and was moved to the Cynan ward, where the officer’s visit occurred. At the inquest in Caernarfon, consultant psychiatrist Dr Olufemi Adebajo said he was "extremely unhappy" about police visits to wards and that such a conversation could have contributed to Lewis’s agitation and distress. He noted he had known Lewis for more than two years and did not consider him a suicide risk. The day before his death, Lewis asked staff for extra medication for agitation, but dosage was not changed because there were no signs of agitation. On the evening of 2 May, Lewis told staff he was going to the shop, and 45 minutes later his body was found in the Menai Strait. The inquest continues.
